Nothing playing
Pick anything with a ▶ — streams free from archive.org

Keyboard

/ or Ctrl K
command palette — search everything
1–9, 0
switch views (0 = the Universe)
n / p
next / previous in queue
s
start a station from what's playing
q
toggle queue
Esc
close panels
?
this help

Honesty marks

↻
link was AI-fabricated in the source doc; replaced with a verified real recording (hover to see the fake)
✎
date corrected from the source book
⚠
no verified recording exists — never fakes a player

Every identifier, rating, and date comes from the QA'd dataset — nothing here is invented. 224/225 entries resolve to a verified archive.org recording.

Add a version

Nominate a night + song for the book. Contributions queue in this browser and sync to the backend automatically once it's configured (SUPABASE in the source + build/supabase-schema.sql) — nothing is lost either way; you can export the queue as JSON.

Welcome to the Book of the Dead

Your book of 225 starred versions, opened into a streaming library, a discovery radio, and the entire archive — every night the band played. Pick a door:

🎧
Listen
The Library — every starred version, streaming free from archive.org. Hit ▶ on anything; the drawer shows every track of the night.
🔭
Explore
The Trip timeline, the Constellation of segues, a song's Evolution across 30 years, the Atlas map, Insights. Every dot clicks through to music.
🕳
Discover
Stations (Pandora with reasons), The Dig (daily doors), and The Universe — all 2,000+ nights, labeled by what's yours.

The status language — one glance, everywhere

★ IN THE BOOK a version you starred  ·  ⬇ ON YOUR SHELF you own the files locally  ·  ∅ UNEXPLORED the road ahead

The player, honestly

▶ streams the real recording in a pop-up panel — pause & scrub live inside that panel. ⏮ ⏭ walk the queue, or the night's tracklist. ■ stops everything. Every link is verified against archive.org; ↻ marks a repaired fabrication and ⚠ never fakes a player.
/ searches everything · ? shortcuts
